# Tempo – Focus Timer & Pomodoro Productivity App

> Tempo is a free, open-source Pomodoro-style focus timer web app built with Next.js, React, and Tailwind CSS. It helps users stay productive through customizable work-break cycles, task management, daily session goals, streak tracking, and motivational quotes. No sign-up required — works instantly in any modern browser.

## What is Tempo?

Tempo is an online Pomodoro focus timer designed for students, developers, writers, and anyone who wants to manage their time more effectively. It implements the Pomodoro technique — focused work intervals followed by short breaks — with a beautiful, minimal interface. Tempo is completely free, works offline, and requires no account to get started.

## Key Features

- **Circular Timer**: A visual SVG-based countdown timer with smooth animations for work and break sessions.
- **Customizable Durations**: Set your own work duration (default 25 min), break duration (default 5 min), and inactivity threshold.
- **Task Tracking**: Create, start, complete, and delete tasks. Track sessions and total time spent per task.
- **Daily Goals & Streaks**: Set a daily session goal and track your streak of consecutive productive days.
- **Auto-Start**: Optionally auto-start the next work session when a break ends.
- **Browser Notifications**: Get notified when a work session or break completes.
- **Motivational Quotes**: Rotating inspirational quotes displayed during sessions to keep you motivated.
- **Dark Mode**: Full light and dark theme support based on system preference.
- **Offline Support**: Works as a Progressive Web App — use Tempo without an internet connection.
- **Cloud Sync**: Optionally sign up to sync settings, tasks, and progress across devices via Supabase.
- **Data Privacy**: All data is stored locally by default. No tracking, no ads.

## How to Use Tempo

1. Visit https://usetempo.app and click "Try without account" or sign up for free.
2. Open Settings to configure your work duration, break duration, and daily session goal.
3. Create tasks you want to focus on.
4. Select a task and press Start to begin a focused work session.
5. When the timer ends, take a break. Tempo handles the transition automatically.
6. Track completed sessions, daily progress, and streaks over time.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is Tempo free?**
A: Yes, Tempo is 100% free with no premium tiers, ads, or hidden costs.

**Q: Do I need an account?**
A: No. You can use Tempo immediately without signing up. Create an account only if you want to sync data across devices.

**Q: Does Tempo work offline?**
A: Yes. Tempo is a Progressive Web App that works fully offline after the first load.

**Q: What is the Pomodoro technique?**
A: The Pomodoro technique is a time management method that alternates focused work intervals (typically 25 minutes) with short breaks (typically 5 minutes). After four cycles, take a longer break. It helps maintain concentration and prevent burnout.

**Q: Can I customize the timer?**
A: Yes. You can adjust work duration, break duration, and daily session goals in the Settings panel.

**Q: How does task tracking work?**
A: Create tasks in the task list, select one before starting the timer, and Tempo automatically logs sessions and time spent on that task.

**Q: Where is my data stored?**
A: By default, all data is stored in your browser's local storage. If you sign up, data is synced to a secure Supabase backend.

**Q: What browsers are supported?**
A: Tempo works in all modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.

## Tech Stack

- Next.js 15 (App Router)
- React 19
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS 4
- Supabase (optional cloud sync)
- Local Storage for offline persistence

## Architecture

- `src/app/layout.tsx` – Root layout with SEO metadata and structured data.
- `src/app/page.tsx` – Landing page with feature overview.
- `src/app/app/page.tsx` – Main app with timer, controls, tasks, and settings.
- `src/app/login/page.tsx` – Authentication page.
- `src/components/CircularTimer.tsx` – SVG-based circular countdown display.
- `src/components/TimerControls.tsx` – Start, pause, and reset buttons.
- `src/components/DailyProgress.tsx` – Daily goal progress bar and streak display.
- `src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx` – Settings modal for durations, goals, notifications.
- `src/components/TaskList.tsx` – Task creation, listing, and time tracking.
- `src/hooks/useTimer.ts` – Core timer logic hook (work/break cycles, callbacks).
- `src/lib/types.ts` – TypeScript type definitions (Settings, Task, TimerStatus, etc.).
- `src/lib/quotes.ts` – Array of motivational quotes.
- `src/lib/storage/` – Storage abstraction layer (local + Supabase).
- `src/lib/supabase/` – Supabase client utilities for auth and cloud sync.

## URL

- Homepage: https://usetempo.app
- App: https://usetempo.app/app
- Blog: https://usetempo.app/blog
- Login: https://usetempo.app/login

## Blog

Tempo publishes articles about the Pomodoro technique, focus strategies, and time management. Blog posts are available at https://usetempo.app/blog.

- [What Is the Pomodoro Technique? A Complete Guide](https://usetempo.app/blog/pomodoro-technique-guide) — 2026-03-01
- [How to Stay Focused While Studying: 7 Proven Strategies](https://usetempo.app/blog/how-to-stay-focused-while-studying) — 2026-03-04
- [Pomodoro vs. Flowtime vs. 52/17: Which Focus Technique Is Best?](https://usetempo.app/blog/pomodoro-vs-flowtime-vs-52-17) — 2026-03-07

For the full text of all blog posts, see: https://usetempo.app/llms-full.txt
